comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## DONE + live-verified (2026-07-01)
Landed via Gerrit change 123 (-> GitHub main d96628c9).
- src/rebar/mirror_guard.py: replication_verdict + ruleset_verdict (full lock contract) + I/O
  fetchers + CLI (python -m rebar.mirror_guard). stdlib-only.
- tests/unit/test_mirror_guard.py: 25 tests (behavioral: in-sync/diverged, locked vs each drift
  mutation; contractual: verdict schema + CLI exit codes; fetcher parsing incl. robust XSSI strip).
- .github/workflows/mirror-guard.yml: scheduled (cron 15m) + manual guardian; permissions
  administration:read; fails -> notification on drift/divergence.
- observability.sh section 5: publishes rebar/host:mirror_out_of_sync (anon Gerrit + git ls-remote,
  no creds). monitoring_ws7.tf: CloudWatch alarm (sustained divergence) wired to rebar-gerrit-alerts SNS.
- LIVE: guardian reports healthy+locked against prod (exit 0); a simulated ruleset drift (added
  RepositoryRole admin bypass) was DETECTED (exit 1, 'must be DeployKey-only'), then REVERTED
  (exit 0); terraform plan confirmed clean after revert.
- Review-bot findings on change 123 remediated: hardened the bash revision grep (tolerate pretty JSON)
  and the Python _strip_xssi (match the robust gerrit_client sibling); added fetcher parsing tests.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: FORCE_CLOSE: close gate(s) bypassed by user approval — no completion/signature attestation was signed. Reason: "Two ACs are OPERATIONAL live-verifications the code-reading completion-verifier cannot see (it searched the repo, found no artifact). Both were performed live this session against production: (1) python -m rebar.mirror_guard --all -> exit 0, replication in sync + ruleset 'locked (deploy-key-only)'; (2) simulated drift = added a RepositoryRole(admin) bypass to ruleset 18402431 -> guardian exit 1 'bypass_actors must be DeployKey-only, found [DeployKey, RepositoryRole]' -> reverted to DeployKey-only -> exit 0; terraform plan re-confirmed 'No changes'. Code + 25 tests landed on main via Gerrit change 123 (d96628c9). Force-closing: operational ACs are not code-verifiable.". Session: d96628c9.

compiled_state.description:
## What
Continuous alerting for the two post-cutover silent-failure modes of the Gerrit->GitHub mirror:
replication failing (GitHub main stops advancing while Gerrit main moves), and the
gerrit-mirror-lock-main ruleset being mutated/deleted out-of-band (drift to unprotected).

## Design (two independent, secretless watchers)
- A tested Python module (src/rebar/mirror_guard.py) holds the verdict LOGIC + I/O + a CLI, driven
  by a scheduled GitHub Actions workflow (the PRIMARY guardian): native GITHUB_TOKEN authenticates
  the GitHub Rulesets API fetch; the Gerrit main SHA is read ANONYMOUSLY (public REST); no new secret.
- A lightweight bash probe in observability.sh is the SECONDARY, house-style replication watcher:
  it compares the anon Gerrit main SHA vs the anon GitHub main SHA (git ls-remote) and publishes a
  CloudWatch metric (no auth, matching the S5 replication probe) -> a Terraform alarm -> SNS.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] src/rebar/mirror_guard.py exposes replication_verdict(gerrit_sha, github_sha) returning an in-sync/diverged verdict.
- [ ] src/rebar/mirror_guard.py exposes ruleset_verdict(ruleset) enforcing the full lock contract (active, target=branch, refs/heads/main, rules update+deletion+non_fast_forward, bypass = DeployKey only).
- [ ] src/rebar/mirror_guard.py provides thin I/O fetchers for the Gerrit main SHA (anon), the GitHub main SHA, and the GitHub ruleset (GITHUB_TOKEN from env).
- [ ] python -m rebar.mirror_guard runs both checks, prints a JSON result, exits non-zero when unhealthy.
- [ ] Behavioral tests cover in-sync vs diverged for replication_verdict.
- [ ] Behavioral tests cover locked vs each ruleset drift mutation (disabled, missing rule, extra bypass, wrong ref, deleted).
- [ ] Contractual tests pin the verdict output schema.
- [ ] Contractual tests pin the CLI exit codes.
- [ ] .github/workflows/mirror-guard.yml runs the CLI on a cron schedule plus manual dispatch.
- [ ] The workflow grants the job permissions: administration: read so the Actions GITHUB_TOKEN can read the repo ruleset; if org policy disallows that, a fine-grained PAT (administration:read) stored as an Actions secret is the documented fallback.
- [ ] The workflow fails (producing a notification) when either check is unhealthy.
- [ ] infra/scripts/observability.sh publishes the rebar/host:mirror_out_of_sync metric via aws cloudwatch put-metric-data (dimensionless), comparing the anon Gerrit and GitHub main SHAs in bash.
- [ ] infra/terraform/monitoring_ws7.tf defines a CloudWatch alarm on that metric wired to the rebar-gerrit-alerts SNS topic.
- [ ] The guardian is verified LIVE reporting healthy plus locked against production.
- [ ] A simulated ruleset drift is detected by the guardian, then reverted.

## Files
- src/rebar/mirror_guard.py, tests/unit/test_mirror_guard.py, .github/workflows/mirror-guard.yml, infra/scripts/observability.sh, infra/terraform/monitoring_ws7.tf
compiled_state.title: Replication-health + ruleset-drift alerting for the Gerrit->GitHub mirror
